Dialog: this is test dialog
Wow, you really were hard to dig out.
But you’re here now. Welcome to the afterlife. You’re in hell.
And I know that’s really confusing-
Not really.
Oh. Huh.
Weird, because your life was pretty ordinary.
I mean, says here there was a lot of gambling.
Like… a lot, but I don’t think gambling really determines your placement.
I mean, literally not a speck of cheating? That would probably determine it a little.
Spent time with friends, drank a lot… I mean all and all very morally neutral and you aren’t even a bit surprised?
But whatever, you can thank me once you’re out of you because I’m getting you to the border.
Here, I pulled these from your soul before you woke up. It’s your essence manifested into a tool to get you through the wilds.
I thought it would be something cool like a big gun or maybe a sword, but uh… cards, huh? Is that cool to you?
It doesn’t matter. Pass it here.
Now I tested them out, so let me show you how to do the do.
Congrats, killer.
Welcome back.
Good ta’ see ya.
Hey, Ace.
Good choice.
Well... if you're sure.
Good luck out there.
The border awaits.
Hey, you got a minute?
Sure. Ain’t in a rush.
See, that’s what I’m curious about.
You die, you end up in hell, but you don’t seem bothered in the slightest… Why?
Why’re you so bothered by it? Ain’t you supposed to be a demon?
What, I can’t be multifaceted just because I’m a demon?
I’ve lived for what is probably centuries to you. I’ve seen a lot of souls enter hell and most of them were pretty scared.
I mean… some were happy. And those ones were REAL freaky.
But you’re the first person I’ve ever seen that seems to be completely unfazed by it.
Why?
I just figured…
I’d be ending up here anyway.
But you didn’t do anything particularly bad.
That so?
Yeah. I mean, I’ve looked over your soul’s deed and it seemed very average.
This may come as a shock to you, but people like you don’t end up here. They get to heaven.
Maybe I’m bad and you just don’t know it. What makes you think your little deed can tell you everything about me?
Well that’s what I want to figure out.
Is it morally wrong by y’alls books to think about hurting somebody?
I mean… I don’t know. I’ve never seen anything like that come up. I guess I never even thought about it.
Hows about you mull that one over while I get back to doing what I’m here to do.
Uh, sure. I’ll do that.
Here, a card for your troubles.
Now that you have a break, let’s chat.
More of this morality nonsense?
Sure. More.. nonsense
So I looked at the books and while thinking about hurting people is generally pretty frowned upon, it’s not going to make as much of an impact as actually hurting somebody
But what if someone wanted to hurt someone really bad?
What if someone wanted to kill somebody?
Would that little book of yours have a scenario for that?
I think the action is probably more important than the thought in the long-run, don’t you?
And what makes you think that? The book?
… Yes?
So you live your life determined to follow the moral rulins that someone else set up for you? You just decide everything that way, do ya?
What? Is there supposed to be some other way?
Maybe. Maybe there isn’t. I ain’t a teacher.

You know, up there in the livin’ world, a whole bunch of people live their lives tellin’ other people that they deserve this place because of what they look like, who they love, everythin’ about who they are.
Everyone got ideas of right and wrong.
Yeah well a lot of those people are also violent and their bigotry ends them up here.
Even if they pray?
Even if they pray. It’s the rules.
And if they don't hurt anyone physically, where do they go?
Uh... I-
What if they only make people feel real bad with their words, or just think it to themself?
I don't know, I guess.
And what if I hurt somebody because they hurt me first? What happens to me.
Uh… well… I mean you did hurt somebody in that scenario-
But they hurt me first. What if it was the only thing between me and death?
… huh. I- I need to look at the books some more I think.
Sure. You have fun with that, Roane.
Here. Take this with you.
Ace-
Okay, before you get into all your yappin, I got a question.
Oh. Sure, go ahead.
Why do you gotta read all these books? Do they not preload you with all the info you need?
No?
We’re born just like all you living things are.
So we learn like you all do. We even have personalities and everything.
Not everyone down here likes being down here.
I mean, some do, but not all.
Interesting.
Anyway, your dilemma with morality and whatnot.
I think I hate these books.
I had no idea being a human was so complicated. I talked to some tortured souls and they all had really complicated lives. It honestly sounds like living is hell.
It is for some people.
So does… any of this matter?
There are some real real bad people in your world, but…
What does torturing them even do?
And what if we torture the wrong people?
Do you want to know how you ended up here?
Sure.
When you died, your soul got confused with someone else's. You were on a street car and when it crashed you both died at the same time.
Your souls got sent to the wrong places and now you’re here and that guy is up there. Some-
Mob boss. I know.
What?
I was coming here no matter what. I figured that I would find him down here, but if he’s up there, then I guess I’ll just have to march my way to heaven to finish the job I wasn’t able to in life.
… What are you trying to say?
I was going to kill him. That’s what I’m saying.
He took everything from my community. He killed the person that used to be my best friend.
So I had to send him to his grave.
And I don’t care if you think it was right or wrong.
If I could go back to that moment, I wouldn’t have hesitated.
Wow, Ace. I don’t really know what to say.
Um. Here, take this.
Thanks.
More questions today?
No.
I think… maybe trying to figure out what is good and bad in all scenarios is hard.
Maybe it’s not something I can understand just by reading my manuals or grilling you.
I don’t think I’m even supposed to say this, but I think this whole system is fucked.
It amazes me that you ain’t even about all the death and killin and torturin.
I bet the demons who like that stuff have a lot of fun. You should try not bein such a stick in the mud.
Ouch.
But then who would ask you overly complicated questions about reality?
And who would you have to help you complete your little mission?
Oh? So now killin isn’t so bad?
I feel like that guy should probably be down here is all.
I have about a century longer to figure out what I think of torture now, but that guy cannot be in heaven. I know that for sure.
Well.
Thank you, Roane.
Huh? Why are you thanking me?
For helping me.
Hey, I’m not just helping you! I’m helping set things right.
And I think you should be able to get off on the technicality of not actually assassinating him.
I mean… think of how many people he would still be torturing if he hadn’t been killed. If it wasn’t for the accident, I guess dying by your hands would’ve been pretty decent karma.
Well, I’m glad you have your own answer on how ya feel.
I think thinking about the morality of all this is hurting my brain, but I know I want to help you. So that’s what I’m going to do.
Take this. Let’s get this show on the road.
(A spectral form in the shape of a coyote stands in front of you. It looks so lifelike you forget for a moment that you’ve died.)
(It stares at you from a few feet away, questioning if it should come closer.)
(It sniffs the air.)
(It steps back, glaring for a moment, then runs away into the foliage nearby.)
What did I do? You came up to me.
(Its glare isn’t improved by your anger.)
If I smell bad, maybe consider that everythin’ down here smells bad. It’s hell. Ain’t much I can do about it.
(It leaves, leaving you alone again.)
(Somehow, letting off some steam by yelling at the coyote makes you feel better, you feel like you can let something go.)
(The spirit stares at you. It sits several feet away from you.)
This again.
What? What do you want?
(It stomps the ground just once, then continues to sit still.)
(You feel compelled to get down to eye level with it. You put one knee to the ground.)
(Its stare is piercing. You can see your reflection deep in its eyes as it approaches.)
(Looking at yourself, you feel uneasy. It’s still you. Nothing has changed. You expected to look in much worse repair, but somehow still looking like yourself doesn’t ease your heart.)
(You see the creases on your face. The anger in your own eyes reflected back at you.)
(It feels like the anger is eating away at you like acid.)
(You’re angry at the world. You’re angry at yourself for not being stronger. You’re angry you died.)
(And you’re sad.)
(And now you’re crying.)
(The coyote finally stops in front of you. It places its chin on your raised knee.)
(You pull the coyote close, holding it as you cry.)
(Letting yourself feel your emotions has made you feel lighter. When you finally get up, you pet the coyote on the head and head off ready to face what’s to come.)
Hey there, you.
(The coyote sniffs the air around you as it approaches, circling you.)
(You kneel down.)
Thank you for before, friend. I guess I really needed a cry.
YIP!
(It rubs up against you as it circles you joyously.)
Strange to see such a friendly animal down here. But I’m glad I get a little peace and quiet.
I think that maybe it’s hard to carry all these feelins.
I wanted more outta my life.
Even when I knew it was gonna end, I had hope that somehow I’d make it through. Maybe fall in love, maybe dance and drink and gamble some more.
Man, I miss cards. Playin them I mean, not… fightin with em.
(The coyote sits and listens intently.)
But I ain’t gettin it back. This is what I got now. I got you, I got Roane, and I got this fight ahead of me.
(You feel a weight lift off of your shoulders. You’re ready to continue.)
(There’s a spirit in this room. A young girl in tattered dress attire.)
Oh! I didn’t see you there. Wow, you look strangely… concrete for someone down. You must have all your memories from when you were alive.
Maybe.
I’ve seen a few other spirits that have more form than I do. I want to get a more solid body like them.
Hey, do you think you could give me a hand?
I know it’s hell and all. For all I know you’re some serial killer or bank robber or just a really mean person who probably doesn’t want to help me.
And for all YOU know I might actually be the super dangerous one.
And I would say, hey, that makes two of us who know nothing about me.
So, help a gal out?
What’s in it for me? Don’t you think someone in hell might be the kind of person to want compensation?
Hm.
Well, there isn’t real money down here as far as I know, and I can’t pick stuff up currently, so… Maybe I can give you something after you help me?
Sure, I’ll take it.
Okay great!
Hm, I feel kinda hazy right now, how about you come back later. We’ll talk then.
(As she vanishes, two cards fall to the ground.)
You came back!
Yup.
Okay, I did some research. We just need to sit here and hold hands.
Not happening.
No! Trust me. Come on, just sit down.
(The two of you sit down cross-legged in front of each other and you hold out your hands. Her hands are passing through yours, which is supposedly holding hands in a way.)
Now close your eyes. We’re going back into my mind. You’re going to have to guide me through my head.
(Suddenly, your eyes are open, yet not open.)
(It’s like you’re lucid dreaming. You and Anne, who is now in full human form, are sitting across from you at an old wooden desk. There are stacks of files in front of you.)

(Each file is labeled with what the file contains. Embarrassing secrets, the entire history of Anne’s bowel movements, every sneeze, every yawn, it’s all cataloged. Most of it you don’t even bother to look at.)
(The two of you spend a lot of time desperately sifting to find something relevant.)
(Then you see it.)
Hey, did you find something? I found my birth records!
(You hesitate, then pass the file over. It’s labeled “Mob affiliation and subsequent death”.)
(You watch as Anne pours over the files and pages. When she’s finally read enough, she gets up and sits a bit away from you in the black void.)
(You walk over and sit down next to her.)
I was a rich girl who ran away from home.
And I didn’t have any money… but I had friends… and I gambled… and I drank… and I danced…
I guess at some point the money I had ran dry. I was in debt and needed to do something to make quick money.
I didn’t want to sell myself, so I dressed up as a man and started working for the mob.
I did… horrible things for them… why did I do any of that? Why was money ever that important?
Why didn’t I just lean on my friends?
(She starts sobbing.)
I killed people. I hurt people. For WHAT?
And all of it just to die in a shoot out. Why? Why did I have to do that?
(She’s shaking and crying.)
I get it now. Why I’m down here.
I… I deserve this.
(You don’t say anything. You don’t know what to say.)
(A small part of you wishes you could say she didn't deserve this, but you can't find it in you to say anything.)
(When you finally awaken from the trance, Anne is sitting in front of you, holding your hands. She quickly bolts up and away from you without another word.)
(Two cards are laying on the ground.)
(She’s back, pacing back and forth.)
Oh. I didn’t think I would see you again.
But then again, maybe you came back because you did something way worse than me and I don’t scare you.
Oh no. You’re definitely worse than me.
(She looks stunned and uncomfortable.)
Huh. I guess that’s fair.
I tried to kill someone. I didn’t end up doing it though.
Oh. And you got down here for that?
Who knows. Could’ve been anything. I’m not dwelling on it.
There’s nothin I can do now.
But don’t you feel miserable?
Sure. I do feel miserable that I’m dead. Not particularly about the attempted murderin though.
But why?
The guy deserved it.
Well I’m sorry I can’t be as relaxed as you! I was a genuinely bad person.
Yeah, you were at one point. You were also a desperate person someone preyed on.
And before all that you were someone else entirely.
That doesn’t excuse anything I did.
No. It don’t.
And I ain’t tellin you to not feel bad about it, but maybe also try to remember who you were before that.
Hell ain’t exactly a great place to start your life for eternity over in, but…
In a way, you got a second chance here to do somethin else.
I guess you’re right.
I just don’t know what to do with myself.
I was so desperate to find the answer to why I was down here because I thought maybe I would find out that secretly… I wasn’t supposed to be here.
But I guess none of that matters. Belong here, don’t belong here, I’m here now and that’s what matters.
So I guess, I’ll have to figure it out from here.
I just wish I could say sorry to my friends. I abandoned them when I should’ve just told them what was happening.
I was so scared they’d tell me to go back to my family and my arranged marriage… but none of them were the type.
I would give anything to remember their faces…
Thank you, Ace. I’ll find my way from here.
Take care, Anne.
(As she vanishes, two cards fall to the ground.)
